problem.title: Longest Common Subsequence
problem.description:
Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence.
                
Definition: A subsequence of a string is a new string generated from the original string with some characters (can be none) deleted without changing the relative order of the remaining characters.
assumptions: Subsequence: characters in order but not necessarily contiguous.

example.explanation: The longest common subsequence is "ace" and its length is 3.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming (Bottom-up)
solutions.description: Use a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the length of LCS of text1[0...i-1] and text2[0...j-1].

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Space Optimized DP
solutions.description: We can optimize space by using only two rows of the DP table.

solutions.title: Solution 3 – Recursive with Memoization
solutions.description: Top-down approach using recursion and memoization:

complexity.space: O(m*n) for memoization + O(m+n) for recursion stack

solutions.title: Printing the LCS
solutions.description: To print the actual LCS, we can backtrack through the DP table:
